reasoning:
The current Treasury General Account (TGA) value (as of Nov 5, 2025) is unusually high at $942.7k, driven by massive mid-to-late October tax receipts and continuous issuance. However, the Treasury's Q4 Quarterly Refunding Announcement (QRA) explicitly set an end-of-December 2025, as well as an end-of-March 2026, cash balance target of $850k. 
Given this policy directive perfectly aligns with standard post-tax mid-quarter TGA drainage, the account is highly expected to drift downwards toward its $850k structural target over the upcoming weeks. 

1. For Nov 16 (the closest resolution date, pointing to the Nov 12 data point), there are no major new note/bond settlements or tax receipts to counteract the daily operational deficits. It is almost certain the TGA will be lower than $942.7k. (Probability: 12%)
2. For Dec 9 (Dec 3 data point), there will be a localized upward spike due to the late Nov / Dec 1 settlements. However, with the steep anticipated drawdown throughout November to meet the low $850k target, it is highly unlikely to breach the prior $942k peak. (Probability: 22%)
3. For Feb 7, 2026, uncertainty is notably higher. January's tax inflows and end-of-month settlements will temporarily swell the balance again. Still, given the structural target remains flat at $850k for Q1 2026, the overall gravity keeps it depressed compared to the high base anchor. (Probability: 36%, returning close to the statistical prior).
